title: PNG to Pixel Art | Image to Pixel Art Converter
metaDescription: Convert PNG to pixel art online. Adjust pixel size and palettes with instant preview, then export crisp results — free and private.
h1: Convert PNG to Pixel Art Online
intro: Use this page when your source file is already a PNG and you want a format-specific start. PNG files usually keep edges, flat color areas, and transparency cleaner than lossy formats, which makes them a strong input for pixel art.

Upload the PNG, tune pixel size and palette, and export a clean result for sprites, icons, tiles, or UI art. If you need the broadest control set for mixed file types or general-purpose work, use the main image to pixel art converter.
toolHeading: Upload a PNG and tune the pixel conversion
toolSubtitle: Best when your source already has clean edges, transparency, or interface-style artwork.
toolSubtitle2: Need the broadest workflow for mixed sources or deeper cleanup? Open the main image to pixel art converter.

topCallout.title: Need the full image to pixel art workflow?
body: This PNG page is a focused starting point, but the main converter is the better destination when you want the complete set of palette, dithering, preview, and export controls.
topCallout.ctaLabel: Open the full image to pixel art converter

bottomCallout.title: Finished testing the PNG workflow?
body: Move into the main converter when you want to compare palette options, refine mixed-source artwork, or keep one consistent workflow across multiple file types.

title: JPG to Pixel Art | Image to Pixel Art Converter
metaDescription: Convert JPG to pixel art online instantly. Fine‑tune pixels and palettes with real-time preview, then export clean results — fast, free, and privacy‑first.
h1: Convert JPG to Pixel Art Online
intro: Use this page when your source is a JPG photo, screenshot, or compressed graphic and you want a focused entry point. JPG files usually need a little more cleanup because compression can soften edges before the pixel conversion even starts.

Upload the JPG, adjust pixel size and palette, then export a cleaner result for avatars, posters, references, or retro graphics. If you want the broadest workflow for mixed sources, use the main image to pixel art converter.

toolSubtitle: A narrower workflow for photo-heavy or compressed JPG sources.
toolSubtitle2: For the full palette, dithering, and export workflow across any file type, use the main image to pixel art converter.

title: Image to Pixel Art Converter | Turn Any Image Into Pixel Art
metaDescription: Turn PNG, JPG, GIF, or WEBP files into pixel art with live preview, palette controls, dithering, and private browser-based processing.
detailsHeading: When to use the main converter
heroSubtitle: Upload PNG, JPG, GIF, or WEBP files and turn them into clean pixel art with live preview, palette controls, and private browser-based processing.
heroSubtitle2: Adjust pixel size, dithering, and colors in the full editor, then download sprites, icons, UI art, and retro graphics.
intro: Use the main converter when you need the full image-to-pixel-art workflow in one place, from upload and live preview to palette control, dithering, and export.
It works best for screenshots, logos, illustrations, interface art, mixed-source graphics, and general-purpose conversions that need more than a narrow file-format landing page.
If your source is mostly a camera photo, start with Photo to Pixel Art. If you need a format-specific workflow, open the PNG, JPG, GIF, or WEBP converter pages from the homepage.

minecraftBlock.title: Convert Image to Minecraft Pixel Art
body: Use the main converter when you want to turn reference art, logos, or screenshots into block-friendly Minecraft pixel art before rebuilding them in-game.
Lower pixel size, tighter palettes, and visible grid structure make it easier to read the result as a block layout instead of a soft illustration.
Start the conversion here, then tune colors and detail until the shape reads clearly at Minecraft build scale.

aiBlock.title: Is this an AI pixel art generator?
body: No. This page is a rule-based image to pixel art converter, not an AI image generator.
That means you keep direct control over the source image, pixel size, palette, dithering, and export result instead of asking a model to invent a new image.
For creators who need repeatable outputs, sprites, icons, and UI art, that control is usually more reliable than an AI-style generation workflow.
